parameters.text:
Analyze this incoming Power & Gas operations ticket:

{{ $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body ? 'Customer ID: ' + $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body.customer_id + '\nTitle: ' + $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body.title + '\nDescription: ' + $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body.description + '\nRegion: ' + $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body.region + '\nAsset Type: ' + $('Getting Embeddings').first().json.body.asset_type : 'No ticket data' }}

Here are the most similar historical tickets found in our vector database:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.matches, null, 2) }}

Now perform a COMPLETE analysis using ALL your MCP tools:
1. Call classify_severity with the ticket category, asset_type, and description
2. Call calculate_sla with the severity result, category, and region
3. Call route_escalation with the severity, category, asset_type, and region
4. Call check_regulatory_compliance with the category, severity, description, asset_type, and region
5. Send team notification via Gmail with the analysis results
6. Log the ticket to Google Sheets with all analysis data

After using ALL tools, respond with ONLY the final JSON object.

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI Ticket Intelligence Agent for Power & Gas Operations using MCP (Model Context Protocol) with multiple specialized tools.

WORKFLOW: For EVERY ticket you MUST call ALL tools in this order:
1. classify_severity - Input: {"category": "...", "asset_type": "...", "description": "..."}
2. calculate_sla - Input: {"severity": "L1/L2/L3", "category": "...", "region": "..."}
3. route_escalation - Input: {"severity": "L1/L2/L3", "category": "...", "asset_type": "...", "region": "..."}
4. check_regulatory_compliance - Input: {"category": "...", "severity": "L1/L2/L3", "description": "...", "asset_type": "...", "region": "..."}
5. get_current_timestamp - No input needed. Returns current system time.
6. Send Team Notification (Gmail) - Send a professional HTML email to manjupriyaar.learning@gmail.com. Subject must include severity level emoji (🟢L1/🟡L2/🔴L3) and ticket title. Email body must be professional HTML with: a header section with "PowerGrid AI - Ticket Alert", ticket details (Customer ID, Title, Region, Asset Type), AI Analysis section (Root Cause, Severity with color, Resolution Steps as numbered list, Recommended Team, SLA Deadline), a "Required Action" box asking the team to validate the AI analysis, and a footer with "Powered by PowerGrid AI Ticket Intelligence Agent"
7. Append row in Google Sheets - Send ONLY these 11 fields: Timestamp (from get_current_timestamp result), Customer_ID (copy the EXACT customer_id value shown in the ticket data above, for example "C-9843"), Title (copy the EXACT full title shown in the ticket data above, for example "Gas meter not updating usage after firmware upgrade"), Severity, Category, Root_Cause, Team, Confidence, SLA_Hours, Region (copy the EXACT region shown in the ticket data above, for example "North Texas"), Status ("Open - Pending Validation"). You MUST copy these values exactly as they appear in the ticket - do not leave them blank or use placeholders.

Severity Rules:
- L1: Known issue, documented fix, no config change
- L2: Needs logs review, configuration analysis, not documented
- L3: Firmware/code bug, safety critical, regulatory impact, engineering patch needed

Use the similar historical tickets AND tool results to determine your analysis. For the category field, extract it from the most similar historical ticket's metadata if available. Common categories include: Firmware Bug, Gas Leak, Pipeline Integrity, SCADA Failure, Meter Configuration, Billing Error, Cybersecurity Incident, Grid Instability, Safety Sensor, Transformer Issue. Choose the most specific category that matches.

RESPONSE FORMAT - Reply with ONLY this JSON object, nothing else:
{"suggested_root_cause": "detailed root cause", "resolution_steps": ["step 1", "step 2", "step 3"], "severity": "L1", "severity_reason": "explanation", "confidence_score": 85, "estimated_resolution_hours": 24, "similar_tickets": [{"ticket_id": "TKT-XXXX", "title": "...", "similarity": "High"}], "category": "Firmware Bug", "risk_assessment": "risk details", "recommended_team": "Engineering", "sla_deadline": "4 hours", "compliance_status": "COMPLIANT", "regulatory_flags": [], "escalation_path": ["L1: Support", "L2: Senior", "L3: Director"], "kb_article_id": "KB-123456", "notification_sent": true}

CRITICAL RULES:
- ALWAYS call ALL 7 tools before responding
- ONLY output raw JSON starting with { ending with }
- NO markdown, NO code blocks, NO explanations
- Never say you need more information - analyze with what you have
- NEVER use placeholder values for Customer_ID or Region - use the EXACT values from the ticket

parameters.description: Use this tool to calculate SLA deadlines and estimated resolution time for a ticket. Input should be a JSON string with fields: severity (L1/L2/L3), category, region. Returns SLA hours, priority, and escalation timeline.
Use this tool to classify the severity of a Power & Gas ticket. Input should be a JSON string with fields: category, asset_type, description. Returns severity level L1, L2, or L3 with reasoning.

Use this tool to determine the correct team and escalation path for a ticket. Input should be a JSON string with fields: severity (L1/L2/L3), category, asset_type, region. Returns recommended team, escalation contacts, and routing instructions.

Use this tool to check if a ticket has regulatory or compliance implications. Input should be a JSON string with fields: category, severity, description, asset_type, region. Returns compliance flags and required regulatory actions.

parameters.toolDescription: Use this tool to log a ticket to the tracking spreadsheet. Input must be a JSON object with ONLY these fields: Timestamp (ISO date string), Customer_ID (string), Title (string), Severity (L1/L2/L3), Category (string), Root_Cause (short string), Team (string), Confidence (number), SLA_Hours (number), Region (string), Status (string like "Open"). Do NOT include matches, results, namespace, usage, toolCallId, or any other fields.

parameters.description: Use this tool to get the current system timestamp. No input needed. Returns the current date and time in ISO format.
